candidates.pageDescription: 2005 U.S. Supreme Court case on capital punishment

candidates.text: Management of the United States Customs Service and the United States Secret Service moves to the United States Department of Homeland Security.
candidates.pageDescription: U.S. federal government agency from 1789 to 2003

candidates.text: U.S. invasion of Afghanistan: Operation Anaconda begins in eastern Afghanistan.
candidates.pageDescription: 2002 military offensive against al-Qaeda and the Taliban during the war in Afghanistan

candidates.text: The Envisat environmental satellite successfully launches aboard an Ariane 5 rocket to reach an orbit of 800 km (500 mi) above the Earth, which was the then-largest payload at 10.5 m long and with a diameter of 4.57 m.
Space Shuttle Columbia is launched on STS-109 to service the Hubble Space Telescope.
Titanic becomes the first film to gross over $1 billion worldwide.
Bosnia and Herzegovina declares its independence from Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.
Uprisings against Saddam Hussein begin in Iraq, leading to the deaths of more than 25,000 people, mostly civilians.
Steve Jackson Games is raided by the United States Secret Service, prompting the later formation of the Electronic Frontier Foundation.
Provisional Irish Republican Army member Bobby Sands begins his hunger strike in HM Prison Maze.
Watergate scandal: Seven are indicted for their role in the Watergate break-in and charged with conspiracy to obstruct justice.
Black September storms the Saudi embassy in Khartoum, Sudan, resulting in the assassination of three Western hostages.
President of Pakistan Yahya Khan indefinitely postpones the pending national assembly session, precipitating massive civil disobedience in East Pakistan.
Venera 3 Soviet space probe crashes on Venus becoming the first spacecraft to land on another planet's surface.
The Ba'ath Party takes power in Syria.
Villarrica Volcano begins a strombolian eruption causing lahars that destroy half of the town of Coñaripe.
candidates.pageDescription: Type of volcanic eruption with relatively mild explosive intensity or spattering

candidates.text: Paradise Airlines Flight 901A crashes near Lake Tahoe, Nevada, killing 85.
candidates.pageDescription: 1964 aviation accident in the United States

candidates.text: American Airlines Flight 1 crashes into Jamaica Bay in New York, killing 95.
candidates.pageDescription: Bay on the southern side of Long Island, New York

candidates.text: Uganda becomes self-governing and holds its first elections.
Samuel Alphonsus Stritch is appointed Pro-Prefect of the Propagation of Faith and thus becomes the first U.S. member of the Roman Curia.
The International Air Transport Association finalizes a draft of the Radiotelephony spelling alphabet for the International Civil Aviation Organization.
Formation of the East German Nationale Volksarmee.

Nuclear weapons testing: The Castle Bravo, a 15-megaton hydrogen bomb, is detonated on Bikini Atoll in the Pacific Ocean, resulting in the worst radioactive contamination ever caused by the United States.
candidates.pageDescription: Controlled detonation of nuclear weapons for scientific or political purposes

candidates.text: Armed Puerto Rican nationalists attack the United States Capitol building, injuring five Representatives.
candidates.pageDescription: Meeting place of the United States Congress

candidates.text: Soviet Premier Joseph Stalin suffers a stroke and collapses; he dies four days later.
candidates.pageDescription: Leader of the Soviet Union from 1924 to 1953

candidates.text: Cold War: Klaus Fuchs is convicted of spying for the Soviet Union by disclosing top secret atomic bomb data.
candidates.pageDescription: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

candidates.text: The International Monetary Fund begins financial operations.
candidates.pageDescription: Financial institution and UN specialized agency

candidates.text: The Bank of England is nationalised.
candidates.pageDescription: Central bank of the United Kingdom

candidates.text: World War II: Japanese forces land on Java, the main island of the Dutch East Indies, at Merak and Banten Bay (Banten), Eretan Wetan (Indramayu) and Kragan (Rembang).
World War II: Bulgaria signs the Tripartite Pact, allying itself with the Axis powers.
An Imperial Japanese Army ammunition dump explodes at Hirakata, Osaka, Japan, killing 94.
candidates.pageDescription: Principal ground force of the Empire of Japan

candidates.text: Aviator Charles Lindbergh's 20-month-old son Charles Jr is kidnapped from his home in East Amwell, New Jersey. His body would not be found until May 12.
The Australian cricket team captained by Warwick Armstrong becomes the first team to complete a whitewash of The Ashes, something that would not be repeated for 86 years.
Following mass protests in Petrograd demanding greater freedom in the RSFSR, the Kronstadt rebellion begins, with sailors and citizens taking up arms against the Bolsheviks.
March 1st Movement begins in Korea under Japanese rule.
candidates.pageDescription: 1910–1945 colony of the Empire of Japan

candidates.text: The Zimmermann Telegram is reprinted in newspapers across the United States after the U.S. government releases its unencrypted text.
China joins the Universal Postal Union.
candidates.pageDescription: Specialized agency of the United Nations

candidates.text: The deadliest avalanche in United States history buries a Great Northern Railway train in northeastern King County, Washington, killing 96 people.
The Australian Army is formed.
candidates.pageDescription: Land warfare branch of the Australian Defence Force

candidates.text: Battle of Adwa: An Ethiopian army defeats an outnumbered Italian force, ending the First Italo-Ethiopian War.
candidates.pageDescription: 1896 battle of the First Italo-Ethiopian War

candidates.text: Henri Becquerel discovers radioactive decay.
Electrical engineer Nikola Tesla gives the first public demonstration of radio in St. Louis, Missouri.
Yellowstone National Park is established as the world's first national park.
candidates.pageDescription: National park in the western United States

candidates.text: The victorious Prussian Army parades through Paris, France, after the end of the Siege of Paris during the Franco-Prussian War.
Marshal F. S. López dies during the Battle of Cerro Corá thus marking the end of the Paraguayan War.
candidates.pageDescription: 1870 final battle of the Paraguayan War

candidates.text: Nebraska is admitted as the 37th U.S. state.
United States President John Tyler signs a bill authorizing the United States to annex the Republic of Texas.
candidates.pageDescription: President of the United States from 1841 to 1845

candidates.text: A convention of delegates from 57 Texas communities convenes in Washington-on-the-Brazos, Texas, to deliberate independence from Mexico.
Napoleon returns to France from his banishment on Elba.
Leaders of the Mamluk dynasty are killed by Egyptian ruler Muhammad Ali.
candidates.pageDescription: Slave-soldiers and enslaved mercenaries in the Muslim world

candidates.text: Justice Samuel Chase is acquitted at the end of his impeachment trial before the U.S. Senate.
candidates.pageDescription: Founding Father of the United States (1741–1811)

candidates.text: The Dutch East India Company is nationalized by the Batavian Republic.
The Articles of Confederation goes into effect in the United States.
candidates.pageDescription: First constitution of the United States from 1781 to 1789

candidates.text: Sarah Good, Sarah Osborne, and Tituba are brought before local magistrates in Salem Village, Massachusetts, beginning what would become known as the Salem witch trials.
candidates.pageDescription: American colonist executed during the Salem Witch Trials (1653–1692)

candidates.text: Samuel de Champlain reclaims his role as commander of New France on behalf of Cardinal Richelieu.
Writs issued in February by Charles I of England mandate that every county in England (not just seaport towns) pay ship tax by this date.
candidates.pageDescription: King of England, Scotland and Ireland from 1625 to 1649

candidates.text: Sixty-three Huguenots are massacred in Wassy, France, marking the start of the French Wars of Religion.
candidates.pageDescription: Historical religious group of French Protestants

candidates.text: Forces of the Catholic Monarchs engage the combined Portuguese-Castilian armies of Afonso V and Prince John at the Battle of Toro.
candidates.pageDescription: 1476 battle of the War of the Castilian Succession near Toro, Spain

candidates.text: University of Coimbra, in Portugal, is officially chartered by King Denis.
Emperor Louis the Pious is restored as sole ruler of the Frankish Empire.
candidates.pageDescription: Emperor of the Carolingian Empire from 813 to 840

candidates.text: Vetranio proclaims himself Caesar after being encouraged to do so by Constantina, sister of Constantius II.

Emperor Diocletian and Maximian appoint Constantius Chlorus and Galerius as Caesars. This is considered the beginning of the Tetrarchy, known as the Quattuor Principes Mundi ("Four Rulers of the World").
Publius Valerius Publicola celebrates the first triumph of the Roman Republic after his victory over the deposed king Lucius Tarquinius Superbus at the Battle of Silva Arsia.

editorialHighlights.label: Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat

candidates.text: International Women's Day marches in Mexico become violent with 62 police officers and 19 civilians injured in Mexico City alone.
candidates.pageDescription: Day to promote women's rights worldwide

candidates.text: Twenty-eight political institutions in Myanmar establish the National Unity Consultative Council, a historic alliance of ethnic armed organizations and democratically elected leaders, in response to the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.

The first Aurat March (social/political demonstration) is held on International Women's Day in Karachi, Pakistan, since then held annually across Pakistan, and the feminist slogan "Mera Jism Meri Marzi" (My body, my choice), in demand for women's right to bodily autonomy and against gender-based violence, came into vogue in Pakistan.
The Azure Window, a natural arch on the Maltese island of Gozo, collapses in stormy weather.
candidates.pageDescription: Former natural arch in Gozo, Malta

candidates.text: In one of aviation's greatest mysteries, Malaysia Airlines Flight 370, carrying a total of 239 people, disappears en route from Kuala Lumpur to Beijing. The fate of the flight remains unknown.

Headlined by Hulk Hogan and Ric Flair, TNA Wrestling moves its flagship program, TNA Impact!, to Monday night. This effort to go "big time live" failed but is notable in the history of professional wrestling television.
A new constitution is signed by Iraq's Governing Council.
Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-102, carrying the Expedition 2 crew to the International Space Station.
A collision at Indira Gandhi International Airport kills 9 people.
Aeroflot Flight 3379 is hijacked by the Ovechkin family and diverted to Veshchevo in the Soviet Union.
candidates.pageDescription: Rural locality in Vyborgsky, Leningrad Oblast, Russia

candidates.text: A supposed failed assassination attempt on Islamic cleric Sayyed Mohammad Hussein Fadlallah in Beirut, Lebanon kills 80 and injures 200 others.
Cold War: While addressing a convention of Evangelicals, U.S. President Ronald Reagan labels the Soviet Union an "evil empire".
candidates.pageDescription: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

candidates.text: Philips demonstrates the compact disc publicly for the first time.
Images taken by Voyager 1 prove the existence of volcanoes on Io, a moon of Jupiter.
Nelson's Pillar in Dublin, Ireland, is destroyed by a bomb.
candidates.pageDescription: Former column and statue in Dublin, Ireland

candidates.text: Vietnam War: US Marines arrive at Da Nang.
Aeroflot Flight 513 crashes during takeoff from Kuybyshev Airport, killing 30 and injuring 9.
The Ba'ath Party comes to power in Syria in a coup d'état.
A Turkish Airlines Fokker F27 Friendship crashes into Mount Medetsiz in the Taurus Mountains of Turkey, killing all 11 people on board.
The iconic Volkswagen Type 2 "Bus" begins production.

President of France Vincent Auriol and ex-Vietnamese emperor Bảo Đại sign the Élysée Accords, giving Vietnam greater independence from France and creating the State of Vietnam to oppose Viet Minh-led Democratic Republic of Vietnam.
World War II: The Dutch East Indies surrender Java to the Imperial Japanese Army.
World War II: Imperial Japanese Army forces capture Rangoon, Burma from the British.
Spanish Civil War: The Battle of Guadalajara begins.
Daytona Beach and Road Course holds its first oval stock car race.
candidates.pageDescription: Motorsport track in the United States

candidates.text: A mine disaster kills 172 coal miners near Castle Gate, Utah.
candidates.pageDescription: Ghost town in Utah, United States

candidates.text: Spanish Prime Minister Eduardo Dato Iradier is assassinated while on his way home from the parliament building in Madrid.
International Women's Day protests in Petrograd mark the beginning of the February Revolution (February 23 in the Julian calendar).
candidates.pageDescription: Day to promote women's rights worldwide

candidates.text: The United States Senate votes to limit filibusters by adopting the cloture rule.
candidates.pageDescription: Upper house of the US Congress

candidates.text: World War I: A British force unsuccessfully attempts to relieve the siege of Kut (present-day Iraq) in the Battle of Dujaila.
French aviatrix Raymonde de Laroche becomes the first woman to receive a pilot's license.
Sakai incident: Japanese samurai kill 11 French sailors in the port of Sakai, Osaka.
candidates.pageDescription: 1868 killing of French sailors by samurai in Japan

candidates.text: King Oscar I ascends to the thrones of Sweden and Norway.
candidates.pageDescription: King of Sweden and Norway from 1844 to 1859

candidates.text: The Althing, the parliament of Iceland, is reopened after 45 years of closure.
War of the Second Coalition: At the Battle of Abukir, a British force under Sir Ralph Abercromby lands in Egypt with the aim of ending the French campaign in Egypt and Syria.
candidates.pageDescription: Second war on revolutionary France by European monarchies

candidates.text: Gnadenhutten massacre: Ninety-six Native Americans in Gnadenhutten, Ohio, who had converted to Christianity, are killed by Pennsylvania militiamen in retaliation for raids carried out by other Indian tribes.
candidates.pageDescription: 1782 killing of Christian Lenape by American soldiers during the Revolutionary War

candidates.text: An anonymous writer, thought by some to be Thomas Paine, publishes "African Slavery in America", the first article in the American colonies calling for the emancipation of slaves and the abolition of slavery.
Nader Shah, founder of the Afsharid dynasty, is crowned Shah of Iran.
The Safavid Empire of Iran is defeated by an army from Afghanistan at the Battle of Gulnabad.
Queen Anne, the younger sister of Mary II, becomes Queen regnant of England, Scotland, and Ireland.
candidates.pageDescription: Queen of England, Scotland, and Ireland from 1689 to 1694

candidates.text: Treaty of Roskilde: After a devastating defeat in the Northern Wars (1655–1661), Frederick III, the King of Denmark–Norway is forced to give up nearly half his territory to Sweden.
candidates.pageDescription: 1658 territorial settlement between Denmark–Norway and Sweden

candidates.text: The city of Pori (Swedish: Björneborg) is founded by Duke John on the shores of the Gulf of Bothnia.
Battle of Hausbergen between bourgeois militias and the army of the bishop of Strasbourg.
candidates.pageDescription: 1262 conflict to free the city of Strasbourg

candidates.text: Following the death of his mother, queen Urraca of León, Alfonso VII is proclaimed king of León.
candidates.pageDescription: Queen of León, Castile, and Galicia from 1109 to 1126

candidates.text: Ferdowsi completes his epic poem Shahnameh.

editorialHighlights.label: Sinatra Snaps the Fight of the Century

candidates.text: Four members of a Palestinian family are shot dead in Tammun by the Israeli military.
candidates.pageDescription: Municipality type B in Tubas, State of Palestine

candidates.text: The 2022 Sri Lankan protests begins amidst Sri Lanka's economic collapse.
candidates.pageDescription: Protests against the Sri Lankan government

candidates.text: Fifty-one people are killed in the Christchurch mosque shootings.
Beginning of the 2019–20 Hong Kong protests.
Approximately 1.4 million young people in 123 countries go on strike to protest climate change.
candidates.pageDescription: International youth movement for climate action

candidates.text: Beginning of the Syrian revolution.
Stockpiles of obsolete ammunition explode at an ex-military ammunition depot in the village of Gërdec, Albania, killing 26 people.
Cold War: The Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany comes into effect, granting full sovereignty to the Federal Republic of Germany.
candidates.pageDescription: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

candidates.text: Mikhail Gorbachev is elected as the first and only President of the Soviet Union.
candidates.pageDescription: Leader of the Soviet Union from 1985 to 1991

candidates.text: Collapse of Hotel New World: Thirty-three people die when the Hotel New World in Singapore collapses.
Somalia and Ethiopia sign a truce to end the Ethio-Somali War.
candidates.pageDescription: Country in the Horn of Africa

candidates.text: Fifteen people are killed when Sterling Airways Flight 901, a Sud Aviation Caravelle, catches fire following a landing gear collapse at Mehrabad International Airport in Tehran, Iran.
candidates.pageDescription: 1974 plane crash and fire in Tehran, Iran

candidates.text: President Lyndon B. Johnson, responding to the Selma crisis, tells the U.S. Congress "We shall overcome" while advocating the Voting Rights Act.
candidates.pageDescription: President of the United States from 1963 to 1969

candidates.text: At the 1961 Commonwealth Prime Ministers' Conference, South Africa announces that it will withdraw from the Commonwealth when the South African Constitution of 1961 comes into effect.
candidates.pageDescription: 11th meeting of the Commonwealth of Nations' heads of government

candidates.text: The Iranian oil industry is nationalized.
candidates.pageTitle: Nationalization of the Iranian oil industry

candidates.text: World War II: Third Battle of Kharkiv: The Germans retake the city of Kharkiv from the Soviet armies.
Germany occupies Czechoslovakia.
Carpatho-Ukraine declares itself an independent republic, but is annexed by Hungary the next day.
candidates.pageDescription: Short-lived autonomous region and unrecognized state (1938–1939)

candidates.text: The first Women's Boat Race between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge takes place on The Isis in Oxford.
candidates.pageDescription: Boat race on the River Thames

candidates.text: After Egypt gains nominal independence from the United Kingdom, Fuad I becomes King of Egypt.
Talaat Pasha, former Grand Vizir of the Ottoman Empire and chief architect of the Armenian genocide, is assassinated in Berlin by a 23-year-old Armenian, Soghomon Tehlirian.
Ukrainian War of Independence: The Kontrrazvedka is established as the counterintelligence division of the Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.
The American Legion is founded.
Finnish Civil War: The battle of Tampere begins.
Tsar Nicholas II of Russia abdicates the Russian throne, ending the 304-year Romanov dynasty.
candidates.pageDescription: Monarchical title in some Slavic countries

candidates.text: The first parliamentary elections of Finland (at the time the Grand Duchy of Finland) are held.
Madeleine bombing by Désiré Pauwels during the Ère des attentats.
The Lobau bombing is one of the first attacks of the Ère des attentats (1892-1894).
Start of the Anglo-Tibetan War of 1888.
candidates.pageDescription: 1888 British military operation to expel Tibetan forces from Sikkim

candidates.text: First ever official cricket test match is played: Australia vs England at the MCG Stadium, in Melbourne, Australia.
Archbishop of New York John McCloskey is named the first cardinal in the United States.
France and Vietnam sign the Second Treaty of Saigon, further recognizing the full sovereignty of France over Cochinchina.
candidates.pageDescription: Historical name for various regions of Vietnam

candidates.text: A revolution breaks out in Hungary, and the Habsburg rulers are compelled to meet the demands of the reform party.
candidates.pageDescription: Failed Hungarian revolt against the Austrian and Russian Empires

candidates.text: Sailor Benjamin Morrell erroneously reports the existence of the island of New South Greenland near Antarctica.
Maine is admitted as the twenty-third U.S. state.
In an emotional speech in Newburgh, New York, George Washington asks his officers not to support the Newburgh Conspiracy. The plea is successful, and the threatened coup d'état never takes place.
candidates.pageDescription: City in New York, United States

candidates.text: The British under Charles Cornwallis defeat American forces under Nathanael Greene in the battle of Guilford Court House.
King Charles II of England issues the Royal Declaration of Indulgence, granting limited religious freedom to all Christians.
candidates.pageDescription: Decree by King Charles II of England granting liberty to all Christians

candidates.text: A dam failure causes the sudden flooding of the mining city of Potosí in present-day Bolivia leading to the death of thousands and the massive release of toxic mercury into the environment.
candidates.pageDescription: Catastrophic failure of dam barrier by uncontrolled release of water

candidates.text: Mughal Emperor Akbar abolishes the jizya tax on non-Muslim subjects.

Treaty of Lublowa: After the Peace of Thorn, Grand Master Heinrich von Plauen asks Sigismund of Hungary for economic aid. Sigismund agrees to mediate reduction to the third installment, demarcation of the Samogitian border, and other matters with a grand tournament. Hunts and lavish feasts were also organized. Sigismund invited, among others, polish king Wladyslaw Jagiello, Heinrich von Plauen and bosnian king Tvrtko II. There were people from 17 countries and languages - 40.000 nobles and 2000 knights were present from all over Europe, even England.
candidates.pageDescription: 27th Grand Master of the Teutonic Knights (1410–1413)

candidates.text: Battle of Halmyros: The Catalan Company defeats Walter V, Count of Brienne to take control of the Duchy of Athens, a Crusader state in Greece.
Afonso I of Portugal captures in a surprise attack the city of Santarem from the Almoravids.
After a ten-year truce, German King Henry the Fowler defeats a Hungarian army at the Battle of Riade near the Unstrut river.
candidates.pageDescription: King of East Francia from 919 to 936

candidates.text: Al-Hadi ila'l-Haqq Yahya enters Sa'dah and founds the Zaydi Imamate of Yemen.
candidates.pageDescription: 9th- and 10th-century Arab religious leader; founder of the Zaydi Imamate in Yemen

candidates.text: Michael III, emperor of the Byzantine Empire, overthrows the regency of his mother, empress Theodora (wife of Theophilos) with support of the Byzantine nobility.
Odoacer, the first barbarian King of Italy after the fall of the Western Roman Empire, is slain by Theoderic the Great, king of the Ostrogoths, while the two kings were feasting together.
Constantius Gallus is elevated as Caesar and then sent to Antioch to govern the Roman East.
The assassination of Julius Caesar, the dictator of the Roman Republic, by a group of senators takes place on the Ides of March.
The Roman Republic under its new consuls Publius Sulpicius Galba and Gaius Aurelius Cotta declares war on Philip V of Macedon, starting the Second Macedonian War.
Roman consul Aulus Manlius Vulso celebrates an ovation for concluding the war against Veii and securing a forty years' truce.

Air Canada Express Flight 8646, a Bombardier CRJ-900 operated by Jazz Aviation, collided with a fire truck while landing at LaGuardia Airport in New York City. Two fatalities were reported, both being the captain and the co-pilot. This marked the first fatal accident involving a CRJ-900.
candidates.pageDescription: 2026 aviation accident in New York, U.S.

candidates.text: At least 151 people are killed and 609 injured in a bombing and mass shooting at the Crocus City Hall in Krasnogorsk, Russia.
candidates.pageDescription: Concert venue in Moscow Oblast, Russia

candidates.text: Ten people are killed in a mass shooting in Boulder, Colorado.
candidates.pageDescription: Home rule city and seat of Boulder County, Colorado, United States

candidates.text: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi announces the country's largest ever self-imposed curfew, in an effort to fight the spread of COVID-19.
Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis announces a national lockdown and the country's first ever self-imposed curfew, in an effort to fight the spread of COVID-19.
The Special Counsel investigation on the 2016 United States presidential election concludes when Robert Mueller submits his report to the United States Attorney General.
Two buses crash in Kitampo, a town north of Ghana's capital Accra, killing at least 50 people.
A terrorist attack in London near the Houses of Parliament leaves four people dead and at least 20 injured.
Syrian civil war: Five hundred members of the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) are airlifted south of the Euphrates by United States Air Force helicopters, beginning the Battle of Tabqa.
Three suicide bombers kill 32 people and injure 316 in the 2016 Brussels bombings at the airport and at the Maelbeek/Maalbeek metro station.
candidates.pageDescription: Islamic State suicide bombings in Belgium

candidates.text: At least 37 people are killed and 200 are injured after a fire destroys a camp containing Burmese refugees near Ban Mae, Thailand.
Three Christian Peacemaker Team (CPT) hostages are freed by British forces in Baghdad after 118 days of captivity and the murder of their colleague from the U.S., Tom Fox.

Ahmed Yassin, co-founder and leader of the Palestinian Sunni Islamist group Hamas, two bodyguards, and nine civilian bystanders are killed in the Gaza Strip when hit by Israeli Air Force Hellfire missiles.
Tara Lipinski, aged 14 years and nine months, becomes the youngest women's World Figure Skating Champion.
Comet Hale–Bopp reaches its closest approach to Earth at 1.315 AU.
NASA's Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on its 16th mission, STS-76.
Cosmonaut Valeri Polyakov returns to earth after setting a record of 438 days in space.
The Intel Corporation ships the first Pentium chips (80586), featuring a 60 MHz clock speed, 100+ MIPS, and a 64 bit data path.
USAir Flight 405 crashes shortly after takeoff from New York City's LaGuardia Airport, leading to a number of studies into the effect that ice has on aircraft.
Fall of communism in Albania: The Democratic Party of Albania wins a decisive majority in the parliamentary election.
candidates.pageDescription: Collapse of communist rule in Albania in the early 1990s

candidates.text: The United States Congress votes to override President Ronald Reagan's veto of the Civil Rights Restoration Act of 1987.
candidates.pageDescription: Bicameral legislature of the United States

candidates.text: NASA's Space Shuttle Columbia is launched from the Kennedy Space Center on its third mission, STS-3.
Karl Wallenda of The Flying Wallendas dies after falling off a tight-rope suspended between two hotels in San Juan, Puerto Rico.
A fire at the Browns Ferry Nuclear Power Plant in Decatur, Alabama, causes a dangerous reduction in cooling water levels.
candidates.pageDescription: City in and county seat of Morgan County, Alabama

candidates.text: The United States Congress sends the Equal Rights Amendment to the states for ratification.
candidates.pageDescription: Proposed amendment to the U.S. Constitution

candidates.text: In Eisenstadt v. Baird, the United States Supreme Court decides that unmarried persons have the right to possess contraceptives.
The Beatles release their debut album Please Please Me.
Arthur Leonard Schawlow and Charles Hard Townes receive the first patent for a laser.
A United States Air Force aircraft disappears with all 67 people on board somewhere over the Pacific Ocean.
candidates.pageDescription: Air service branch of the U.S. military

candidates.text: A United States Navy Douglas R6D-1 Liftmaster crashes into Hawaii's Waiʻanae Range, killing 66.
candidates.pageDescription: Maritime service branch of the U.S. military

candidates.text: The United Kingdom grants full independence to Transjordan.
World War II: The city of Hildesheim, Germany, is heavily damaged in a British air raid, though it had little military significance and Germany was on the verge of final defeat.
The Arab League is founded when a charter is adopted in Cairo, Egypt.
World War II: The entire village of Khatyn (in present-day Republic of Belarus) is burnt alive by Schutzmannschaft Battalion 118.
World War II: In the Mediterranean Sea, the Royal Navy confronts Italy's Regia Marina in the Second Battle of Sirte.
Germany takes Memel from Lithuania.
The first Masters Tournament is held at Augusta National Golf Club in Georgia.
candidates.pageDescription: Golf course in Georgia, United States

candidates.text: Cullen–Harrison Act: President Franklin D. Roosevelt signs an amendment to the Volstead Act, legalizing the manufacture and sale of "3.2 beer" (3.2% alcohol by weight, approximately 4% alcohol by volume) and light wines.
candidates.pageDescription: 1933 U.S. legislation which legalized low-alcohol beer and wine

candidates.text: Nazi Germany opens its first concentration camp, Dachau.
Azeri and Turkish army soldiers with participation of Kurdish gangs attack the Armenian inhabitants of Shushi (Nagorno Karabakh).
Yuan Shikai abdicates as Emperor of China, restoring the Republic and returning to the Presidency.
candidates.pageDescription: First president of the Republic of China (1859–1916)

candidates.text: Mystic Phan Xích Long, the self-proclaimed Emperor of Vietnam, is arrested for organising a revolt against the colonial rule of French Indochina, which was nevertheless carried out by his supporters the following day.
The first England vs France rugby union match is played at Parc des Princes in Paris.
Charilaos Vasilakos wins the first modern Olympic marathon race with a time of three hours and 18 minutes.
Before the Société pour L'Encouragement à l'Industrie, brothers Auguste and Louis Lumière demonstrate movie film technology publicly for the first time.
candidates.pageDescription: Visual art consisting of moving images

candidates.text: The Stanley Cup ice hockey competition is held for the first time, in Montreal, Canada.
The Spanish National Assembly abolishes slavery in Puerto Rico.
In North Carolina, William Woods Holden becomes the first governor of a U.S. state to be removed from office by impeachment.
The Austrians defeat the Piedmontese at the Battle of Novara.
In the London Protocol, the three protecting powers (United Kingdom, France and Russia) establish the borders of Greece.
The Slave Trade Act of 1794 bans the export of slaves from the United States, and prohibits American citizens from outfitting a ship for the purpose of importing slaves.
candidates.pageDescription: U.S. law that limits involvement in the international slave trade

candidates.text: Battle of Croix-des-Bouquets: Black slave insurgents gain a victory in the first major battle of the Haitian Revolution.
The Emerald Buddha is moved with great ceremony to its current location in Wat Phra Kaew, Thailand.
The British Parliament passes the Stamp Act that introduces a tax to be levied directly on its American colonies.
Nader Shah occupies Delhi in India and sacks the city, stealing the jewels of the Peacock Throne.
Notable Privateer Henry Morgan lands in Cuba to raid and plunder the inland town of Puerto del Príncipe during the latter stages of the Anglo-Spanish War (1654–1660).
candidates.pageDescription: Person or ship engaging in maritime warfare under commission

candidates.text: Anne Hutchinson is expelled from Massachusetts Bay Colony for religious dissent.
The Massachusetts Bay Colony outlaws the possession of cards, dice, and gaming tables.
Jamestown massacre: Algonquians kill 347 English settlers around Jamestown, Virginia, a third of the colony's population, during the Second Anglo-Powhatan War.
candidates.pageDescription: Town and fort established in the Virginia Colony

candidates.text: The Pilgrims of Plymouth Colony, led by governor John Carver, sign a peace treaty with Massasoit, sachem of the Wampanoags; Squanto serves as an interpreter between the two sides.
Ferdinand II of Aragon commissions Amerigo Vespucci chief navigator of the Spanish Empire.
Vox in excelso: Pope Clement V dissolves the Order of the Knights Templar.
candidates.pageDescription: Bull issued by Pope Clement V in 1312

candidates.text: Battle of Yashima: the Japanese forces of the Taira clan are defeated by the Minamoto clan.
Æthelred of Wessex is defeated by a Danish invasion army at the Battle of Marton.
Roman emperor Severus Alexander is murdered, marking the start of the Crisis of the Third Century.
Start of the Bostran era, the calendar of the province of Arabia Petraea.
candidates.pageDescription: Calendar era of the Roman province of Arabia Petraea

editorialHighlights.label: Westminster Bridge Defies a King and the Church

candidates.text: The Artemis II lunar flyby mission launches, marking the first crewed flight above low Earth orbit since Apollo 17 in 1972.
The 2016 Nagorno-Karabakh conflict begins along the Nagorno-Karabakh Line of Contact.
candidates.pageDescription: April 2016 conflict in the territory of Nagorno-Karabakh

candidates.text: After protests against the burning of the Quran turn violent, a mob attacks a United Nations compound in Mazar-i-Sharif, Afghanistan, resulting in the deaths of fourteen people, including seven UN workers.
Serious Organised Crime Agency (SOCA) of the Government of the United Kingdom is enforced, but later merged into National Crime Agency on 7 October 2013.
candidates.pageDescription: Non-departmental public body of the UK government from 2006-13

candidates.text: Google launches its Email service Gmail.

An EP-3E United States Navy surveillance aircraft collides with a Chinese People's Liberation Army Shenyang J-8 fighter jet. The Chinese pilot ejected but is subsequently lost. The Navy crew makes an emergency landing in Hainan, China and is detained.
candidates.pageDescription: Maritime service branch of the U.S. military

candidates.text: Former President of Federal Republic of Yugoslavia Slobodan Milošević surrenders to police special forces, to be tried on war crimes charges.
Same-sex marriage becomes legal in the Netherlands, the first contemporary country to allow it.
candidates.pageDescription: Country in Northwestern Europe and the Caribbean

candidates.text: Nunavut is established as a Canadian territory carved out of the eastern part of the Northwest Territories.
Comet Hale–Bopp is seen passing at perihelion.
NASCAR champion Alan Kulwicki is killed in a plane crash near the Tri-Cities Regional Airport in Blountville, Tennessee.
Margaret Thatcher's new local government tax, the Community Charge (commonly known as the "poll tax"), is introduced in Scotland.
candidates.pageDescription: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1979 to 1990

candidates.text: Communist Party of Nepal (Mashal) cadres attack a number of police stations in Kathmandu, seeking to incite a popular rebellion.
candidates.pageDescription: Far-left political party in Nepal from 1984 to 1991

candidates.text: Singer Marvin Gaye is shot to death by his father in his home in Arlington Heights, Los Angeles, California.
Iran becomes an Islamic republic by a 99% vote, officially overthrowing the Shah.
Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple Computer, Inc.
The Local Government Act 1972 of England and Wales comes into effect.
candidates.pageDescription: Act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom

candidates.text: Project Tiger, a tiger conservation project, is launched in the Jim Corbett National Park, India.
Bangladesh Liberation War: The Pakistan Army massacre more than a thousand people in Keraniganj Upazila, Bangladesh.
President Richard Nixon signs the Public Health Cigarette Smoking Act into law.
candidates.pageDescription: President of the United States from 1969 to 1974

candidates.text: A Royal Air Maroc Sud Aviation Caravelle crashes near Berrechid, Morocco, killing 61.
The Hawker Siddeley Harrier, the first operational fighter aircraft with Vertical/Short Takeoff and Landing capabilities, enters service with the Royal Air Force.
The British Admiralty, War Office and Air Ministry are replaced by a unified Defence Council of the United Kingdom.
The TIROS-1 satellite transmits the first television picture from space.
The EOKA rebellion against the British Empire begins in Cyprus, with the goal of unifying with Greece.
candidates.pageDescription: 1955–1959 Greek nationalist guerrilla organisation in Cyprus

candidates.text: United States President Dwight D. Eisenhower authorizes the creation of the United States Air Force Academy in Colorado Springs, Colorado.
candidates.pageDescription: World War II general, U.S. president from 1953 to 1961

candidates.text: The Government of Canada repeals Japanese-Canadian internment after seven years.
Cold War: Communist forces respond to the introduction of the Deutsche Mark by attempting to force the western powers to withdraw from Berlin.
candidates.pageDescription: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

candidates.text: Faroe Islands gain autonomy from Denmark.
The only mutiny in the history of the Royal New Zealand Navy begins.
candidates.pageDescription: Maritime arm of the New Zealand Defence Force

candidates.text: The 8.6 Mw Aleutian Islands earthquake shakes the Aleutian Islands with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VI (Strong). A destructive tsunami reaches the Hawaiian Islands resulting in dozens of deaths, mostly in Hilo, Hawaii.
candidates.pageDescription: Chain of islands in the Pacific Ocean

candidates.text: The Malayan Union is established. Protests from locals led to the establishment of the Federation of Malaya two years later.
candidates.pageDescription: Post-war federation of the Malay states and the Straits Settlements of Penang and Malacca

candidates.text: World War II: The Tenth United States Army attacks the Thirty-Second Japanese Army on Okinawa.
World War II: Navigation errors lead to an accidental American bombing of the Swiss city of Schaffhausen.
Fântâna Albă massacre: Between two hundred and two thousand Romanian civilians are killed by Soviet Border Troops.
candidates.pageDescription: 1941 mass killing of ethnic Romanians by Soviet troops at the Romanian–Soviet border

candidates.text: A military coup in Iraq overthrows the regime of 'Abd al-Ilah and installs Rashid Ali al-Gaylani as Prime Minister.
Spanish Civil War: Generalísimo Francisco Franco of the Spanish State announces the end of the Spanish Civil War, when the last of the Republican forces surrender.
Aden becomes a British crown colony.
The Royal New Zealand Air Force is formed as an independent service.
candidates.pageDescription: Air force component of the New Zealand Defence Force

candidates.text: India's central banking institution, the Reserve Bank of India, is formed.
The recently elected Nazis under Julius Streicher organize a one-day boycott of all Jewish-owned businesses in Germany, ushering in a series of anti-Semitic acts.
Adolf Hitler is sentenced to five years fortress confinement for his participation in the "Beer Hall Putsch" but spends only nine months in jail.
The Royal Canadian Air Force is formed.
candidates.pageDescription: Air and space component of the Canadian Armed Forces

candidates.text: In newly formed Northern Ireland, six Catholics are murdered in the Arnon Street killings, one week after six others were killed in the McMahon killings.
The Royal Air Force is created by the merger of the Royal Flying Corps and the Royal Naval Air Service.
candidates.pageDescription: Air and space warfare force of the United Kingdom

candidates.text: The Territorial Force (renamed Territorial Army in 1920) is formed as a volunteer reserve component of the British Army.
Prince George becomes absolute monarch of the Cretan State.
candidates.pageDescription: 1898–1913 autonomous Ottoman state on the Greek island of Crete

candidates.text: The White Star steamer SS Atlantic sinks off Nova Scotia, killing 547 in one of the worst marine disasters of the 19th century.
Singapore becomes a British crown colony.

American Civil War: Union troops led by Philip Sheridan decisively defeat Confederate troops led by George Pickett, cutting the Army of Northern Virginia's last supply line during the Siege of Petersburg.
The Convention of 1833, a political gathering of settlers in Mexican Texas to help draft a series of petitions to the Mexican government, begins in San Felipe de Austin.
In New York City, the United States House of Representatives achieves its first quorum and elects Frederick Muhlenberg of Pennsylvania as its first Speaker.
candidates.pageDescription: Lower house of the U.S. Congress

candidates.text: J. S. Bach's later Easter Oratorio in its first version is performed at the Nikolaikirche in Leipzig on Easter Sunday.
In the Eighty Years' War, the Watergeuzen capture Brielle from the Seventeen Provinces, gaining the first foothold on land for what would become the Dutch Republic.
Alexios I Komnenos overthrows the Byzantine emperor Nikephoros III Botaneiates, and, after his troops spend three days extensively looting Constantinople, is formally crowned on April 4.
Byzantine Emperor Justin I names his nephew Justinian I as co-ruler and successor to the throne.
Roman emperor Diocletian names Maximian his co-emperor ("Augustus").

editorialHighlights.label: Khomeini Calls for an Islamic Republic

candidates.text: Operation Eternal Darkness: Hours after a ceasefire to the Iran war, Israeli attacks on Lebanon kill at least 357 and injures more than 1200 people in the largest airstrikes of the Lebanon war.
Solar eclipse: A total solar eclipse takes place at the Moon's ascending node, visible across North America.
candidates.pageDescription: Event wherein the Sun is obscured by the Moon

candidates.text: Bernie Sanders ends his presidential campaign, leaving Joe Biden as the Democratic Party's nominee.
Windows XP reaches its standard End Of Life and is no longer supported.
U.S. President Barack Obama and Russian President Dmitry Medvedev sign the New START Treaty.
candidates.pageDescription: President of the United States from 2009 to 2017

candidates.text: A solar eclipse occurs, visible over areas of the Pacific Ocean and Latin American countries such as Costa Rica, Panama, Colombia and Venezuela.

The Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched on mission STS-110, carrying the S0 truss to the International Space Station. Astronaut Jerry L. Ross also becomes the first person to fly on seven spaceflights.
The Space Shuttle Discovery is launched on mission STS-56.
The conservative New Democracy party of Constantine Mitsotakis is elected in the Greek parliamentary election.
Voyageurs National Park is established by the U.S. Congress.
candidates.pageDescription: National park in Minnesota, United States

candidates.text: Hank Aaron passes Babe Ruth as the all-time leader in career home runs by hitting his 715th home run off of Al Downing at Atlanta-Fulton County Stadium.
Bahr El-Baqar primary school bombing: Israeli bombers strike an Egyptian school. Forty-six children are killed.
candidates.pageDescription: 1970 bombing of a school in an Israeli Air Force strike

candidates.text: BOAC Flight 712 catches fire shortly after takeoff. As a result of her actions in the accident, Barbara Jane Harrison is awarded a posthumous George Cross, the only GC awarded to a woman in peacetime.
The Netherlands and West Germany sign an agreement to negotiate the return of German land annexed by the Dutch in return for 280 million German marks as Wiedergutmachung.
candidates.pageDescription: Country in Northwestern Europe and the Caribbean

candidates.text: A team of computer manufacturers, users, and university people led by Grace Hopper meets to discuss the creation of a new programming language that would be called COBOL.
candidates.pageDescription: U.S. naval officer and computer scientist (1906–1992)

candidates.text: The Organization of American States drafts an agreement to create the Inter-American Development Bank.
A Royal Canadian Air Force Canadair Harvard collides with a Trans-Canada Airlines Canadair North Star over Moose Jaw, Saskatchewan, killing 37 people.
South African Airways Flight 201: A de Havilland DH.106 Comet 1 crashes into the sea during night killing 21 people.
candidates.pageDescription: 1954 aviation accident in the Mediterranean Sea

candidates.text: The Central Committee of the Mongolian People's Revolutionary Party elects Yumjaagiin Tsedenbal as General Secretary, marking the beginning of his 44-year-long tenure as de facto leader of Mongolia.
The Australian state of Western Australia votes to secede from the federation in a non-binding referendum, although efforts to implement the result prove unsuccessful.
The French Third Republic and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland sign the Entente cordiale.
In Pollock v. Farmers' Loan & Trust Co. the Supreme Court of the United States declares unapportioned income tax to be unconstitutional.
candidates.pageTitle: Pollock v. Farmers' Loan & Trust Co.
candidates.pageDescription: 1895 U.S. Supreme Court case on the constitutionality of federal income taxes

candidates.text: William Ewart Gladstone introduces the first Irish Home Rule Bill into the British House of Commons.
Austro-Prussian War: Italy and Prussia sign a secret alliance against the Austrian Empire.
Black Hawk War: Around 300 United States 6th Infantry troops leave St. Louis, Missouri to fight the Sauk Native Americans.
candidates.pageDescription: 1832 conflict between the United States and Native Americans

candidates.text: The Venus de Milo is discovered on the Aegean island of Milos.
candidates.pageDescription: Ancient Greek marble statue of Aphrodite

candidates.text: Czar Alexander I, the Russian Emperor and the Grand Duke of Finland, officially announces the transfer of the status of the Finnish capital from Turku to Helsinki.
candidates.pageDescription: Title of the ruler of Finland

candidates.text: Shearith Israel, the first synagogue in continental North America, is dedicated.
candidates.pageDescription: Place of worship for Jews or Samaritans

candidates.text: The city of Oulu, Finland, is founded by Charles IX of Sweden.
Fall of Krak des Chevaliers: In Syria, sultan Baibars conquers the Krak des Chevaliers.
Seventh Crusade: Ayyubids of Egypt capture King Louis IX of France in the Battle of Fariskur.
candidates.pageDescription: Religious crusade in Egypt from 1248 to 1254

candidates.text: Mongol–Jin War: The Mongols begin their siege on Kaifeng, the capital of the Jin dynasty.
candidates.pageTitle: Mongol conquest of the Jin dynasty

candidates.text: Manuel I Komnenos succeeds his father John II Komnenos as Byzantine Emperor.
Roger II of Sicily is excommunicated by Innocent II for supporting Anacletus II as pope for seven years, even though Roger had already publicly recognized Innocent's claim to the papacy.
The Battle of Dayr al-'Aqul saves Baghdad from the Saffarids.
Roman emperor Caracalla is assassinated and is succeeded by his Praetorian Guard prefect, Marcus Opellius Macrinus.

editorialHighlights.label: 9/11 Attack on NY's World Trade Towers

candidates.text: Ten people and the perpetrator are killed and twelve others injured in a mass shooting at a school in Onikişubat, Turkey.
candidates.pageDescription: District and municipality in Kahramanmaraş, Turkey

candidates.text: A mass shooting occurs at a FedEx Ground facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, killing nine and injuring seven.
The cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris in France is seriously damaged by a large fire.
In the worst massacre of the South Sudanese Civil War, more than 400 civilians are gunned down after seeking refuge in houses of worship as well as hospitals.
Two bombs explode near the finish line at the Boston Marathon in Boston, Massachusetts, killing three people and injuring over 500 others.
candidates.pageDescription: Capital and largest city in Massachusetts, United States

candidates.text: A wave of bombings across Iraq kills at least 75 people.
candidates.pageDescription: 2013 series of nationwide bombings and shootings in Iraq during the post-U.S. insurgency

candidates.text: Air China Flight 129 crashes on approach to Gimhae International Airport in Busan, South Korea, killing 129 people.
Marrakesh Agreement relating to foundation of World Trade Organization is adopted.
candidates.pageDescription: April 1994 international free trade agreement signed in Marrakech, Morocco

candidates.text: Hillsborough disaster: A human crush occurs at Hillsborough Stadium, home of Sheffield Wednesday, in the FA Cup Semi-final, resulting in the deaths of 97 Liverpool fans.
candidates.pageDescription: Fatal crowd crush during 1989 FA Cup semi-final

candidates.text: Upon Hu Yaobang's death, the Tiananmen Square protests of 1989 begin in China.
The United States launches Operation El Dorado Canyon, its bombing raids against Libyan targets in response to a discotheque bombing in West Germany that killed two U.S. servicemen.
candidates.pageDescription: US April 1986 military operation in Libya

candidates.text: During the Cambodian Civil War, massacre of the Vietnamese minority results in 800 bodies flowing down the Mekong river into South Vietnam.
The EC-121 shootdown incident: North Korea shoots down a United States Navy aircraft over the Sea of Japan, killing all 31 on board.

At Shaw University in Raleigh, North Carolina, Ella Baker leads a conference that results in the creation of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, one of the principal organizations of the civil rights movement in the 1960s.
candidates.pageDescription: Historically Black private college in Raleigh, North Carolina, US

candidates.text: McDonald's restaurant dates its founding to the opening of a franchised restaurant by Ray Kroc, in Des Plaines, Illinois.
First flight of the Boeing B-52 Stratofortress.
Jackie Robinson debuts for the Brooklyn Dodgers, breaking baseball's color line.
Bergen-Belsen concentration camp is liberated.
World War II: The George Cross is awarded "to the island fortress of Malta" by King George VI.
candidates.pageTitle: Award of the George Cross to Malta
candidates.pageDescription: 1942 award by King George VI to the people of Malta

candidates.text: World War II: In the Belfast Blitz, 200 bombers of the German Luftwaffe attack Belfast, killing some 1,000 people.
First day of the Arab revolt in Mandatory Palestine.
Insulin becomes generally available for use by people with diabetes.
Racially motivated Nihon Shōgakkō fire lit by a serial arsonist kills 10 children in Sacramento, California.
U.S. Senator John B. Kendrick of Wyoming introduces a resolution calling for an investigation of a secret land deal, which leads to the discovery of the Teapot Dome scandal.
Two security guards are murdered during a robbery in South Braintree, Massachusetts. Anarchists Sacco and Vanzetti would be convicted of and executed for the crime, amid much controversy.
The British passenger liner RMS Titanic sinks in the North Atlantic at 2:20 a.m., two hours and forty minutes after hitting an iceberg. Only 710 of 2,224 passengers and crew on board survive.
candidates.pageDescription: British passenger liner that sank in 1912

candidates.text: Philippine–American War: Filipino guerrillas launch a surprise attack on U.S. infantry and begin a four-day siege of Catubig, Philippines.
Closing ceremony of the Games of the I Olympiad in Athens, Greece.
candidates.pageDescription: Capital and largest city of Greece

candidates.text: The General Electric Company is formed.
President Abraham Lincoln dies after being shot the previous evening by actor John Wilkes Booth. Vice President Andrew Johnson becomes president.
President Abraham Lincoln calls for 75,000 militiamen to quell the insurrection that soon became the American Civil War.
candidates.pageDescription: President of the United States from 1861 to 1865

candidates.text: Thomas Hopkins Gallaudet and Laurent Clerc found the American School for the Deaf (then called the Connecticut Asylum for the Education and Instruction of Deaf and Dumb Persons), the first American school for deaf students, in Hartford, Connecticut.
Samuel Johnson's A Dictionary of the English Language is published in London.
Serse, an Italian opera by George Frideric Handel, receives its premiere performance in London, England.
Foundation of the short-lived Kingdom of Corsica.
The Pocotaligo Massacre triggers the start of the Yamasee War in colonial South Carolina.
candidates.pageDescription: War between Carolinian settlers and Native tribes in 1700s

candidates.text: Irish Confederate Wars: A Confederate Irish militia is routed in the Battle of Kilrush when it attempts to halt the progress of a Royalist Army.
candidates.pageDescription: Ethno-religious conflict within Ireland between 1641 and 1653

candidates.text: Battle of Rain: Swedes under Gustavus Adolphus defeat the Holy Roman Empire during the Thirty Years' War.
candidates.pageDescription: 1632 battle of the Thirty Years' War

candidates.text: Battle of Formigny: Toward the end of the Hundred Years' War, the French attack and nearly annihilate English forces, ending English domination in Northern France.
candidates.pageDescription: 1450 battle of the Hundred Years' War

candidates.text: The Mamluk Sultanate defeats the Mongols in the battle of Elbistan.
candidates.pageDescription: State in Egypt, Hejaz and Syria (1250–1517)

candidates.text: Henry VI, King of Germany, is crowned Holy Roman Emperor by pope Celestine III.
candidates.pageDescription: Ruler of the Holy Roman Empire from 800 to 1806

candidates.text: Bari, the last Byzantine possession in southern Italy, is surrendered to the Norman Robert Guiscard.
The sharif of Mecca changes the khutbah from the Shiite Fatimid caliph in Cairo to the Sunni Abbasid Caliph in Bagdhad.
candidates.pageDescription: Leader of the Sharifate of Mecca

candidates.text: The Lateran Council ends by condemning the Council of Hieria and anathematizing its iconoclastic rulings.

editorialHighlights.label: Personal Union of Portugal and Spain
A Dictionary of the English Language

candidates.text: At least 26 people are killed in a terrorist attack on a group of tourists in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir. The Resistance Front (TRF), an offshoot of the Pakistan-based militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba claimed responsibility for the attack.
candidates.pageDescription: Town in Jammu and Kashmir, India

candidates.text: Four police officers are killed after being struck by a truck on the Eastern Freeway in Melbourne while speaking to a speeding driver, marking the largest loss of police lives in Victoria Police history.
The Paris Agreement is signed, an agreement to help fight global warming.
Japan's Prime Minister Junichiro Koizumi apologizes for Japan's war record.
candidates.pageDescription: Prime Minister of Japan from 2001 to 2006

candidates.text: Eighteen-year-old Stephen Lawrence is murdered in a racially motivated attack while waiting for a bus in Well Hall, Eltham.
A series of gas explosions rip through the streets in Guadalajara, Mexico, killing 206.
candidates.pageDescription: Ignition of air and flammable gas

candidates.text: Optical fiber is first used to carry live telephone traffic.
Pan Am Flight 812 crashes on approach to Ngurah Rai International Airport in Denpasar, Bali, Indonesia, killing all 107 people on board.
The first Earth Day is celebrated.
Chicano residents in San Diego, California occupy a site under the Coronado Bridge, leading to the creation of Chicano Park.
British yachtsman Sir Robin Knox-Johnston wins the Sunday Times Golden Globe Race and completes the first solo non-stop circumnavigation of the world.
The formation of the Communist Party of India (Marxist–Leninist) is announced at a mass rally in Calcutta.
candidates.pageDescription: Far-left political party in India from 1969 to 1972

candidates.text: American Flyers Airline Flight 280/D crashes on approach to Ardmore Municipal Airport in Ardmore, Oklahoma, killing 83.
Red Scare: Witnesses begin testifying and live television coverage of the Army–McCarthy hearings begins.
Korean War: The Chinese People's Volunteer Army begin assaulting positions defended by the Royal Australian Regiment and the Princess Patricia's Canadian Light Infantry at the Battle of Kapyong.
Arab–Israeli War: The port city of Haifa is captured by Jewish forces.
World War II: Prisoners at the Jasenovac concentration camp revolt. Five hundred twenty are killed and around eighty escape.
candidates.pageDescription: Concentration camp run by the Ustaše in occupied Yugoslavia during World War II

candidates.text: World War II: Sachsenhausen concentration camp is liberated by soldiers of the Red Army and Polish First Army.
candidates.pageDescription: Nazi concentration camp in Oranienburg, Germany

candidates.text: World War II: The 1st Air Commando Group using Sikorsky R-4 helicopters stage the first use of helicopters in combat with combat search and rescue operations in the China Burma India Theater.
World War II: In Greenland, the Allied Sledge Patrol attack the German Bassgeiger weather station.
World War II: Operation Persecution is initiated: Allied forces land in the Hollandia (currently known as Jayapura) area of New Guinea.
candidates.pageDescription: Capital and largest city of Papua, Indonesia

candidates.text: The United Kingdom, Japan and the United States sign the London Naval Treaty regulating submarine warfare and limiting shipbuilding.
World War I: The use of poison gas in World War I escalates when chlorine gas is released as a chemical weapon in the Second Battle of Ypres.
The 1906 Intercalated Games open in Athens.
Spanish–American War: President William McKinley calls for 125,000 volunteers to join the National Guard and fight in Cuba, while Congress more than doubles regular Army forces to 65,000.
candidates.pageDescription: 1898 conflict between Spain and the United States

candidates.text: At noon, thousands rush to claim land in the Land Rush of 1889. Within hours the cities of Oklahoma City and Guthrie are formed with populations of at least 10,000.
The first National League baseball game is played at the Jefferson Street Grounds in Philadelphia.
candidates.pageDescription: Former baseball field in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, US (1864–90)

candidates.text: The U.S. Congress passes the Coinage Act of 1864 that permitted the inscription In God We Trust be placed on all coins minted as United States currency.
candidates.pageDescription: 1864 U.S. federal legislation regulating one- and two-cent coins

candidates.text: Texas Revolution: A day after the Battle of San Jacinto, forces under Texas General Sam Houston identify Mexican General Antonio López de Santa Anna among the captives of the battle when some of his fellow soldiers mistakenly give away his identity.
The second day of the Battle of Eckmühl: The Austrian army is defeated by the First French Empire army led by Napoleon and driven over the Danube in Regensburg.
candidates.pageDescription: 1809 battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition

candidates.text: Treaty of Zaragoza divides the eastern hemisphere between Spain and Portugal along a line 297.5 leagues (1,250 kilometres (780 mi)) east of the Moluccas.
candidates.pageDescription: 1529 peace treaty between Spain and Portugal

candidates.text: Spanish conquistador Hernán Cortés establishes a settlement at Veracruz, Mexico.
candidates.pageDescription: Spanish and Portuguese colonizers of the Age of Discovery

candidates.text: Portuguese navigator Pedro Álvares Cabral lands in Brazil (discovery of Brazil).
Election of Pope Gregory VII following the death of Pope Alexander II.
candidates.pageDescription: Head of the Catholic Church from 1073 to 1085

candidates.text: Basil II is crowned co-Emperor of the Byzantine Empire.

editorialHighlights.label: A Son, A Son – My Wife For A Son!

candidates.text: After a ransomware attack by the group ShinyHunters, the education platform Canvas operated by private company Instructure, goes offline, affecting thousands of educational institutions.
The 2024 Loblaw boycott, a Canadian boycott against retail corporation and grocer Loblaw Companies, begins.
Naxalite attack in Gadchiroli district of India: Sixteen army soldiers, including a driver, killed in an IED blast. Naxals targeted an anti-Naxal operations team.
Naruhito ascends to the throne of Japan succeeding his father Akihito, beginning the Reiwa period.
Syrian civil war: The Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) resumes the Deir ez-Zor campaign in order to clear the remnants of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) from the Iraq–Syria border.
Pope John Paul II is beatified by his successor, Pope Benedict XVI.
candidates.pageDescription: Head of the Catholic Church from 2005 to 2013

candidates.text: Faisal Shahzad attempts to detonate a car bomb in Times Square, but the bomb fails to go off.
Same-sex marriage in Sweden is legalized.
Cyprus, Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Slovakia, and Slovenia join the European Union, celebrated at the residence of the Irish President in Dublin.
candidates.pageDescription: Island country in the Mediterranean Sea

candidates.text: Invasion of Iraq: In what becomes known as the "Mission Accomplished" speech, on board the USS Abraham Lincoln (off the coast of California), U.S. President George W. Bush declares that "major combat operations in Iraq have ended".
The Labour Party wins the 1997 General Election and Tony Blair is elected as Prime Minister.
candidates.pageDescription: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1997 to 2007

candidates.text: Three-time Formula One champion Ayrton Senna is killed in an accident during the San Marino Grand Prix.
Sri Lankan President Ranasinghe Premadasa is assassinated in Colombo in a suicide bombing carried out by the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam.
candidates.pageDescription: President of Sri Lanka from 1989 to 1993

candidates.text: Angolan Civil War: The MPLA and UNITA agree to the Bicesse Accords, which are formally signed on May 31 in Lisbon.
Operation Black Buck: The Royal Air Force attacks the Argentine Air Force during the Falklands War.
candidates.pageDescription: Series of British long-range airstrikes during the Falklands War

candidates.text: Vietnam War: A student anti-war strike erupts in response to U.S. and South Vietnamese forces attacking Vietnamese communists in a Cambodian Campaign.
Cold War: U-2 incident: Francis Gary Powers, in a Lockheed U-2 spyplane, is shot down over the Sverdlovsk Oblast, Soviet Union, sparking a diplomatic crisis.
candidates.pageDescription: 1947–1991 geopolitical rivalry between US and USSR

candidates.text: A Vickers VC.1 Viking crashes while attempting to return to Blackbushe Airport in Yateley, killing 34.
Portella della Ginestra massacre against May Day celebrations in Sicily by the bandit and separatist leader Salvatore Giuliano where 11 persons are killed and 33 wounded.
candidates.pageDescription: 1947 mass killing by Sicilian separatists in Sicily, Italy

candidates.text: Start of three-year Pilbara strike of Indigenous Australians.
candidates.pageDescription: Workers strike in the 1940s in Western Australia

candidates.text: World War II: German radio broadcasts news of Adolf Hitler's death, falsely stating that he has "fallen at his command post in the Reich Chancellery fighting to the last breath against Bolshevism and for Germany". The Soviet flag is raised over the Reich Chancellery, by order of Stalin. Up to 2,500 people die in a mass suicide in Demmin following the advance of the Red Army.
The 7.2 Mw Kopet Dag earthquake shakes the Iran–Turkmenistan border region with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent), killing up to 3,800 and injuring 1,121.
candidates.pageDescription: Earthquake centered on the Iran-Turkmenistan border

candidates.text: The Jaffa riots begin.
The Scofield Mine disaster kills over 200 men in Scofield, Utah in what is to date the fifth-worst mining accident in United States history.
candidates.pageDescription: 1900 explosion of a coal mine in Utah

candidates.text: Spanish–American War: Battle of Manila Bay: The Asiatic Squadron of the United States Navy destroys the Pacific Squadron of the Spanish Navy after a seven-hour battle. Spain loses all seven of its ships, and 381 Spanish sailors die. There are no American vessel losses or combat deaths.
candidates.pageDescription: 1898 conflict between Spain and the United States

candidates.text: Coxey's Army, the first significant American protest march, arrives in Washington, D.C.
The 1891 New Orleans Lynchings: 11 Italian immigrants are lynched by a mob in Louisiana, USA after the acquittal of several defendants in a murder case, the largest mass lynching in U.S. history.
candidates.pageDescription: Murder of eleven Italian men by a mob

candidates.text: On Labor Day in Catania, Sicily, workers establish the first successful Fascio Siciliano, launching a movement of peasants and laborers protesting poverty, landlordism, taxation, and economic hardship across Sicily.
candidates.pageDescription: Federal holiday in the United States

candidates.text: Rallies are held throughout the United States demanding the eight-hour work day, culminating in the Haymarket affair in Chicago, in commemoration of which May 1 is celebrated as International Workers' Day in many countries.
The Memphis Race Riots begin. Over three days, 46 blacks and two whites were killed. Reports of the atrocities influenced passage of the Fourteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution.
candidates.pageTitle: Fourteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
candidates.pageDescription: 1868 amendment addressing citizenship rights and civil and political liberties

candidates.text: The Empire of Brazil, Argentina, and Uruguay sign the Treaty of the Triple Alliance.
American Civil War: The Battle of Chancellorsville between Robert E. Lee's Confederate Army of Northern Virginia and the Union Army of the Potomac under Joseph Hooker begins.
American Civil War: During the Vicksburg campaign, Union forces under Ulysses S. Grant win the Battle of Port Gibson and establish a firm presence on the east side of the Mississippi River.
candidates.pageDescription: Military campaign of the American Civil War

candidates.text: Queen Victoria opens The Great Exhibition at The Crystal Palace in London.
candidates.pageDescription: Queen of the United Kingdom from 1837 to 1901

candidates.text: Execution of the Cato Street Conspirators, who plotted to kill the British Cabinet and Prime Minister Lord Liverpool.
candidates.pageDescription: 1820 planned assassination attempt on UK Government ministers

candidates.text: The Slave Trade Act 1807 takes effect, abolishing the slave trade within the British Empire.
candidates.pageDescription: Act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom

candidates.text: Publication of Species Plantarum by Linnaeus, and the formal start date of plant taxonomy adopted by the International Code of Botanical Nomenclature.
The Act of Union joining England and Scotland to form the Kingdom of Great Britain takes effect.
Henry Morgan's raid on Lake Maracaibo: the Spanish Armada de Barlovento is defeated by an English Privateer fleet led by Captain Henry Morgan.
candidates.pageTitle: Henry Morgan's raid on Lake Maracaibo

candidates.text: The Edict of Expulsion is officially proclaimed in Castile, requiring all Jewish residents to leave within three months.
Scottish Royal forces loyal to King James II defeat a rebel army of the Black Douglases in the battle of Arkinholm.
Wars of Scottish Independence end: By the Treaty of Edinburgh–Northampton, England recognises Scotland as an independent state.
candidates.pageDescription: War of national liberation between Scotland and England

candidates.text: Norman mercenaries land at Bannow Bay in Leinster, marking the beginning of the Norman invasion of Ireland.
A synod in Carthage condemns Pelagianism.

editorialHighlights.label: Soviets Shoot Down US Spy Plane

candidates.text: The 2025 papal conclave elects Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost, taking the name Leo XIV as the 267th Pope of the Catholic Church.
A car bomb explodes in front of a school in Kabul, capital city of Afghanistan killing at least 55 people and wounding over 150.
candidates.pageDescription: Capital and the largest city of Afghanistan

candidates.text: British 17-year-old Isabelle Holdaway is reported to be the first patient ever to receive a genetically modified phage therapy to treat a drug-resistant infection.
candidates.pageDescription: Therapeutic use of bacteriophages to treat bacterial infections

candidates.text: China Southern Airlines Flight 3456 crashes on approach into Bao'an International Airport, killing 35 people.
A fire at Illinois Bell's Hinsdale Central Office triggers an extended 1AESS network outage once considered to be the "worst telecommunications disaster in US telephone industry history".
candidates.pageDescription: Telecommunications utility company in Illinois, USA

candidates.text: The SAS kills eight Provisional Irish Republican Army volunteers and a civilian during an ambush in Loughgall, Northern Ireland.

Corporal Denis Lortie enters the Quebec National Assembly and opens fire, killing three people and wounding 13. René Jalbert, Sergeant-at-Arms of the Assembly, succeeds in calming him, for which he will later receive the Cross of Valour.
The Soviet Union announces a boycott upon the Summer Olympics at Los Angeles, later joined by 14 other countries.
The Thames Barrier is officially opened, preventing the floodplain of most of Greater London from being flooded except under extreme circumstances.
candidates.pageDescription: Flood defence system for London, England

candidates.text: The World Health Organization confirms the eradication of smallpox.
candidates.pageDescription: Specialized agency of the United Nations

candidates.text: The first ascent of Mount Everest without supplemental oxygen, by Reinhold Messner and Peter Habeler.
The rollercoaster The New Revolution, the first steel coaster with a vertical loop, opens at Six Flags Magic Mountain.
A 71-day standoff between federal authorities and the American Indian Movement members occupying the Pine Ridge Reservation at Wounded Knee, South Dakota ends with the surrender of the militants.
Vietnam War: U.S. President Richard Nixon announces his order to place naval mines in major North Vietnamese ports in order to stem the flow of weapons and other goods to that nation.
The Beatles release their 12th and final studio album Let It Be.
The Philippine province of Davao is split into three: Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, and Davao Oriental.

South Vietnamese soldiers under the Roman Catholic President Ngo Dinh Diem open fire on Buddhists defying a ban on the flying of the Buddhist flag on Vesak, killing nine and sparking the Buddhist crisis.
candidates.pageDescription: Buddhist festival marking the birth, enlightenment and death of the Buddha

candidates.text: South Vietnamese President Ngo Dinh Diem begins a state visit to the United States, his regime's main sponsor.
candidates.pageDescription: President of South Vietnam from 1955 to 1963

candidates.text: The Tollund Man is discovered in a peat bog near Silkeborg, Denmark.
candidates.pageDescription: Iron Age bog body from Denmark

candidates.text: Estonian schoolgirls Aili Jõgi and Ageeda Paavel blow up the Soviet memorial which preceded the Bronze Soldier of Tallinn.
candidates.pageDescription: Estonian schoolgirl known for her resistance to Soviet occupation following WWII

candidates.text: World War II: The German Instrument of Surrender signed at Berlin-Karlshorst comes into effect. This is commemorated as Victory in Europe Day.
End of the Prague uprising, celebrated now as a national holiday in the Czech Republic.
candidates.pageDescription: Partially successful 1945 rebellion in German-occupied Czechoslovakia

candidates.text: Hundreds of Algerian civilians are killed by French Army soldiers in the Sétif massacre.
candidates.pageDescription: 1945 mass murder of Muslims in French Algeria

candidates.text: The Halifax riot starts when thousands of civilians and servicemen rampage through Halifax, Nova Scotia.
candidates.pageDescription: 1945 rioting and looting by civilians and servicemen in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada

candidates.text: World War II: The German 11th Army begins Operation Trappenjagd (Bustard Hunt) and destroys the bridgehead of the three Soviet armies defending the Kerch Peninsula.
candidates.pageDescription: Unit of the German Army in the Eastern Front of World War II

candidates.text: World War II: The Battle of the Coral Sea comes to an end with Imperial Japanese Navy aircraft carrier aircraft attacking and sinking the United States Navy aircraft carrier USS Lexington.
candidates.pageDescription: Major naval battle in the Pacific Theater of World War II

candidates.text: World War II: Gunners of the Ceylon Garrison Artillery on Horsburgh Island in the Cocos Islands rebel in the Cocos Islands Mutiny. Their mutiny is crushed and three of them are executed, the only British Commonwealth soldiers to be executed for mutiny during the Second World War.
World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches a bombing raid on Nottingham and Derby.
Mohandas Gandhi begins a 21-day fast of self-purification and launched a one-year campaign to help the Harijan movement.
Attempting to make the first non-stop transatlantic flight from Paris to New York, French war heroes Charles Nungesser and François Coli disappear after taking off aboard The White Bird biplane.
candidates.pageDescription: Flight of an aircraft across the Atlantic Ocean

candidates.text: The Klaipėda Convention is signed formally incorporating Klaipėda Region (Memel Territory) into Lithuania. It would come back to Germany in 1939.
candidates.pageDescription: 1924 territorial settlement between Lithuania and the Conference of Ambassadors

candidates.text: The creation of the Communist Party of Romania.
Edward George Honey proposes the idea of a moment of silence to commemorate the Armistice of 11 November 1918 which ended World War I.
In Martinique, Mount Pelée erupts, destroying the town of Saint-Pierre and killing over 30,000 people. Only a handful of residents survive the blast.
candidates.pageDescription: Active volcano on the Caribbean island of Martinique

candidates.text: The first games of the Italian football league system are played.
candidates.pageDescription: League system for association football in Italy

candidates.text: Pharmacist John Pemberton first sells a carbonated beverage named "Coca-Cola" as a patent medicine.
At Gilmore's Gardens in New York City, the first Westminster Kennel Club Dog Show opens.
candidates.pageDescription: Annual conformation show in New York City

candidates.text: Mexican–American War: American forces led by Zachary Taylor defeat a Mexican force north of the Rio Grande in the first major battle of the war.
candidates.pageDescription: 1846–1848 conflict between Mexico and the United States

candidates.text: A train derails and catches fire in Paris, killing between 52 and 200 people.
Greek War of Independence: The Greeks defeat the Turks at the Battle of Gravia Inn.
candidates.pageDescription: Greek rebellion against the Ottoman Empire (1821–1829)

candidates.text: Branded a traitor during the Reign of Terror, French chemist Antoine Lavoisier, who was also a tax collector with the Ferme générale, is tried, convicted and guillotined in one day in Paris.
King Louis XVI of France attempts to impose the reforms of Étienne Charles de Loménie de Brienne by abolishing the parlements.
candidates.pageTitle: Étienne Charles de Loménie de Brienne

candidates.text: In the Papal States, Cardinal Michelangelo dei Conti is elected Pope, and takes the name Innocent XIII.
candidates.pageDescription: Italian state ruled by the pope (756–1870)

candidates.text: William Coddington founds Newport, Rhode Island.
candidates.pageDescription: Magistrate of the Massachusetts Bay Colony

candidates.text: A newly nationalized silver mine in Scotland at Hilderston, West Lothian is re-opened by Bevis Bulmer.

Hernando de Soto stops near present-day Walls, Mississippi, and sees the Mississippi River (then known by the Spanish as Río de Espíritu Santo, the name given to it by Alonso Álvarez de Pineda in 1519).
A group of imperial guards, led by Trịnh Duy Sản, murder Emperor Lê Tương Dực and flee, leaving the capital Thăng Long undefended.
The Hundred Years War: Joan of Arc lifts the Siege of Orléans, turning the tide of the war
Julian of Norwich, a Christian mystic and anchoress, experiences the deathbed visions described in her Revelations of Divine Love.
The Hundred Years War: the Treaty of Brétigny is drafted between King Edward III of England and King John II of France (the Good).
Reccared I opens the Third Council of Toledo, marking the entry of Visigothic Spain into the Catholic Church.
Emperor Honorius signs an edict providing tax relief for the Italian provinces Tuscia, Campania, Picenum, Samnium, Apulia, Lucania and Calabria, which were plundered by the Visigoths.
Spring and Autumn period: The house of Zhao defeats the house of Zhi, ending the Battle of Jinyang, a military conflict between the elite families of the State of Jin.
candidates.pageDescription: Period in Chinese history (c. 770 – c. 481 BCE)

candidates.text: Prime Minister of Slovakia Robert Fico is shot and critically injured while meeting with supporters at an event in Handlová.
An upsurge in violence in Iraq leaves more than 389 people dead over three days.
Jessica Watson becomes the youngest person to sail, non-stop and unassisted around the world solo.
California becomes the second U.S. state after Massachusetts in 2004 to legalize same-sex marriage after the state's own Supreme Court rules a previous ban unconstitutional.
candidates.pageDescription: Constituent polity of the United States

candidates.text: Arsenal F.C. go an entire league campaign unbeaten in the English Premier League, joining Preston North End F.C. with the right to claim the title "The Invincibles".

A CSX EMD SD40-2 8888 rolls out of a train yard in Walbridge, Ohio, with 47 freight cars, including some tank cars with flammable chemical, after its engineer fails to reboard it after setting a yard switch. It travels south driverless for 66 miles (106 km) until it is brought to a halt near Kenton. The incident became the inspiration for the 2010 film Unstoppable.
